claim: The 1X Neo humanoid robot (first mass-market pre-orderable humanoid) is still heavily teleoperated for most use cases at launch, with human operators on standby to complete tasks the robot cannot handle autonomously
actionable: When evaluating early commercial humanoid robots, expect heavy teleoperation requirements and budget for human operator infrastructure even for products marketed as autonomous
source_title: AI News: 1x Neo Robot, Extropic TSU, Minimax M2, Cursor 2, and more!

claim: Minimax M2, an open-source Chinese model with only 10B active parameters out of 200B total, achieves 61 on the artificial analysis intelligence index, exceeding Gemini 2.5 Pro
actionable: Track open-source model efficiency benchmarks; efficient MoE architectures can achieve state-of-the-art performance with selective parameter activation
source_title: AI News: 1x Neo Robot, Extropic TSU, Minimax M2, Cursor 2, and more!

claim: Elon Musk proposed using idle compute in Tesla vehicles to create a distributed inference fleet; with 100 million cars each providing 1kW inference, this would equal 100 gigawatts of distributed compute
actionable: Consider distributed edge inference architectures that leverage existing compute infrastructure in consumer devices as a scalable alternative to centralized data centers
source_title: AI News: 1x Neo Robot, Extropic TSU, Minimax M2, Cursor 2, and more!

claim: Cursor 2.0's Composer model is 4x faster than similarly-capable models and is designed for low-latency agentic coding with built-in code review and browser testing capabilities
actionable: Prioritize AI coding tools optimized for latency over raw capability for iterative development workflows where speed impacts developer productivity
source_title: AI News: 1x Neo Robot, Extropic TSU, Minimax M2, Cursor 2, and more!
